data_coverage_summary: Webhooks API is Motive's outbound event-delivery surface for near-real-time operational notifications into external systems.
buyer_guidance: Use Webhooks API when the requirement is pushing Motive event payloads into downstream systems with low latency, not querying asset inventory or writing actions back to Motive.
overview: Motive's Webhooks API is an outbound event-delivery mechanism. It can deliver operational event payloads into another platform, which makes telemetry delivery Supported, but it does not expose asset master data and it is not a writeback path into Motive. Asset data is Not Supported. Telemetry data is Supported. Writeback is Not Supported.

available_data.description: The webhook surface is intended for near-real-time delivery of Motive operational signals into subscriber endpoints.

ingest_considerations: Webhook consumers should implement authentication, replay protection, and idempotency because the integration pattern is push-based.
Webhook delivery confirms telemetry flow, but it is not a substitute for an asset master-data API or a writeback channel.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Classification is based on the Motive Open API reference and the captured product summary describing webhook delivery for near-real-time operational event streaming. Outbound events count as telemetry delivery, but not as writeback into Motive.
